title: jenkins pipeline error from podman in podman: error in copier subprocess: error changing to intended-new-root directory \"/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/5d...f1/merged\": chdir /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/5d...f1/merged: invalid argument
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug I thinks it is bug /kind feature **Description** I have create pipeline in jenkins for auto build image by podman, part of the pipeline code: ``` podTemplate( label: 'image-build', cloud: 'kubernetes', containers: [ containerTemplate( name: 'build-tools', image: 'quay.io/containers/podman:latest', command: 'cat', privileged: true, ttyEnabled: true ) ], volumes: [ hostPathVolume(mountPath: '/var/lib/containers', hostPath: '/var/lib/containers') ]) ``` but have error when used podman to build images by Dockerfile: ``` TASK [build test images ] *** task path: /home/jenkins/agent/workspace/test-auto-deploy/auto_build.yml:1 fatal: [localhost]: FAILED! => {"changed": false, "msg": "Failed to build image harbor.test.com:7443/test-nginx:v1.0.0_20210420: Error: error building at STEP \"WORKDIR /usr/share/nginx\": error in copier subprocess: error changing to intended-new-root directory \"/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/5dc9f8ddcc4f1a0ebe6322ea614d64581f4724c0bd6fd9d9d195976a1aafa3f1/merged\": chdir /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/5dc9f8ddcc4f1a0ebe6322ea614d64581f4724c0bd6fd9d9d195976a1aafa3f1/merged: invalid argument\n"} ``` ``` cat auto_build.yml - name: build test images containers.podman.podman_image: name: "{{Registry_Host}}/{{Profile_Name}}/{{NGINX_Service_Name}}" tag: "{{NewVersion}}" push: yes username: "{{Login_Username}}" password: "{{Login_Password}}" path: "{{Code_Folder}}" build: format: docker push_args: compress: yes dest: "{{Registry_Host}}/{{Profile_Name}}/{{NGINX_Service_Name}}:{{NewVersion}}" validate_certs: no ``` ``` cat Dockerfile FROM nginx WORKDIR /usr/share/nginx RUN echo '

hello, docker!

' > /usr/share/nginx/html/index.html ``` I can confirm that the Dockerfile and auto_build.yml was works **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1.create pipeline in jenkins include podTemplate 2.install ansible etc. in pod 3.part of pipeline code for stage ``` stage('auto-build-images') { try { if (fileExists ('ansible/auto_build.yml')) { sh "ansible-playbook -vv ansible/auto_build.yml" } } catch (err) { throw err } } ``` **Describe the results you received:** **Describe the results you expected:** **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** jenkins deploy in k8s of 1.20 ``` kubectl version Client Version: version.Info{Major:"1", Minor:"20", GitVersion:"v1.20.4", GitCommit:"e87da0bd6e03ec3fea7933c4b5263d151aafd07c", GitTreeState:"clean", BuildDate:"2021-02-18T16:12:00Z", GoVersion:"go1.15.8", Compiler:"gc", Platform:"linux/amd64"} Server Version: version.Info{Major:"1", Minor:"20", GitVersion:"v1.20.2", GitCommit:"faecb196815e248d3ecfb03c680a4507229c2a56", GitTreeState:"clean", BuildDate:"2021-01-13T13:20:00Z", GoVersion:"go1.15.5", Compiler:"gc", Platform:"linux/amd64"} ``` **Output of `podman version`:** ``` Version: 1.6.4 RemoteAPI Version: 1 Go Version: go1.12.12 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** ``` debug: compiler: gc git commit: "" go version: go1.12.12 podman version: 1.6.4 host: BuildahVersion: 1.11.5 CgroupVersion: v1 Conmon: package: conmon-2.0.8-1.el7.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.8, commit: f85c8b1ce77b73bcd48b2d802396321217008762' Distribution: distribution: '"rhel"' version: "7.9" MemFree: 8863678464 MemTotal: 16654729216 OCIRuntime: name: runc package: containerd.io-1.4.4-3.1.el7.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/runc version: |- runc version 1.0.0-rc93 commit: 12644e614e25b05da6fd08a38ffa0cfe1903fdec spec: 1.0.2-dev go: go1.13.15 libseccomp: 2.3.1 SwapFree: 0 SwapTotal: 0 arch: amd64 cpus: 16 eventlogger: journald hostname: master1.ioccx.ibm.com kernel: 3.10.0-1160.15.2.el7.x86_64 os: linux rootless: false uptime: 1012h 39m 44.62s (Approximately 42.17 days) registries: blocked: null insecure: null search: - registry.access.redhat.com - registry.redhat.io - docker.io store: ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf ContainerStore: number: 0 GraphDriverName: overlay GraphOptions: {} GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage GraphStatus: Backing Filesystem: xfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" ImageStore: number: 1 RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes ``` **Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** check in master and node for `rpm -q` ``` podman-1.6.4-29.el7_9.x86_64 ``` podman info in quay.io/containers/podman:latest ``` host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.20.0 cgroupManager: cgroupfs cgroupVersion: v1 conmon: package: conmon-2.0.27-2.fc33.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.27, commit: ' cpus: 16 distribution: distribution: fedora version: "33" eventLogger: file hostname: ioccx-build-wwmrh-9z4gt idMappings: gidmap: null uidmap: null kernel: 3.10.0-1160.15.2.el7.x86_64 linkmode: dynamic memFree: 6415421440 memTotal: 16654729216 ociRuntime: name: crun package: crun-0.19-1.fc33.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 0.19 commit: e67a75672412975916dac6b87f8346f870e4b99a spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL os: linux remoteSocket: path: /run/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: false seccompEnabled: true selinuxEnabled: false slirp4netns: executable: "" package: "" version: "" swapFree: 0 swapTotal: 0 uptime: 1011h 32m 0.16s (Approximately 42.12 days) registries: search: - registry.fedoraproject.org - registry.access.redhat.com - registry.centos.org - docker.io store: configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 0 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 0 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: overlay.imagestore: /var/lib/shared overlay.mount_program: Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs Package: fuse-overlayfs-1.5.0-1.fc33.x86_64 Version: |- fusermount3 version: 3.9.3 fuse-overlayfs: version 1.5 FUSE library version 3.9.3 using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31 overlay.mountopt: nodev,fsync=0 graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: xfs Native Overlay Diff: "false" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageStore: number: 1 runRoot: /run/containers/storage volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 3.1.0 Built: 1618238356 BuiltTime: Mon Apr 12 14:39:16 2021 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.15.8 OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 3.1.0 ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?** No **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Please try a more recent Podman version. v3.0 and up featured an extensive rework of copying, for example.

Also, your `podman info` and package version don't match at all - one is from EL7, the other from F33?

@mheon I have tried update to the latest version for podman from EL7,
`podman version`
```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Built:        Mon Mar  1 22:30:40 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
`podman info`
```

But still this error from pipeline running

@rhatdan You ever seen this one before? Looks like podman-in-container, but an EINVAL seems wierd (chdir manpage doesn't even document it).

I would suspect a permissions issue - entirely possible mounting filesystems is blocked by the other container - but that doesn't seem like it would cause an EINVAL, unless mount is set to return cleanly with Seccomp despite being denied?

The problem is most likely caused by some new fields specified on the mount point that RHEL7 kernel does not understand.

zzguang520  Could you check the mount options in storage.conf?

@rhatdan  @mheon  `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf | grep -v -E "^#|^$"` in RHEL7
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
]
[storage.options.overlay]
mountopt = "nodev"
[storage.options.thinpool]
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@zzguang520 Were you able to get any further?
@giuseppe Any ideas?  I would think there might be a flag in overlay mounts that are using a new option not understood by RHEL7 kernel?

I'm hitting the same EINVAL error with buildah 1.21.0 on ubuntu 20.04 with kernel 4.8.0-58-generic when running `buildah run alpine-working-container sh`, both rootless and root -- a simpler test like that with buildah might make troubleshooting easier for OP.  

And no, I don't know yet what's causing it either, though I am also suspecting a flags thing similar to that suggested by @rhatdan.  But here's an excerpt from my own strace -- the mount calls themselves are actually returning zero, though I don't yet know if that 0x1b5 syscall is a factor:

```
683474 mount("/usr/bin/buildah", "/tmp/containers.ZXQqAj", NULL, MS_BIND, NULL) = 0
683474 mount(NULL, "/tmp/containers.ZXQqAj", NULL, MS_RDONLY|MS_REMOUNT|MS_BIND, NULL) = 0
683474 syscall_0x1b5(0xffffffffffffff9c, 0xc0005d2cf0, 0xc00025e808, 0x18, 0, 0) = -1 ENOSYS (Function not implemented)
683474 mount("/home/stevegt/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay", "/home/stevegt/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay", 0xc0002ea200, MS_BIND, NULL) = 0
683474 mount("", "/home/stevegt/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay", 0xc0002ea206, MS_PRIVATE, NULL) = 0
683474 --- SIGURG {si_signo=SIGURG, si_code=SI_TKILL, si_pid=683474, si_uid=0} ---
683483 syscall_0x1b5(0x4, 0x55ccaf7a44e0, 0x7ffc04c0c600, 0x18, 0, 0x20800) = -1 ENOSYS (Function not implemented)
683483 mount("fuse-overlayfs", "/home/stevegt/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/dcbd0c051fa67abd6c7c3007e7bffbcbc21bed77ceddecf0ec0781fb57abeaa1/merged", "fuse.fuse-over
layfs", MS_NODEV|MS_NOATIME, "default_permissions,allow_other,"...) = 0
```

(Later:)  Okay, not sure if this is it, but in the same strace, umount2() is throwing an EINVAL:
```
[pid 687328] chdir("/home/stevegt/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/dcbd0c051fa67abd6c7c3007e7bffbcbc21bed77ceddecf0ec0781fb57abeaa1") = 0
[pid 687288] <... nanosleep resumed>NULL) = 0
[pid 687328] getcwd( <unfinished ...>
[pid 687288] nanosleep({tv_sec=0, tv_nsec=160000},  <unfinished ...>
[pid 687328] <... getcwd resumed>"/home/stevegt/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/dcbd0c051fa67abd6c7c3007e7bffbcbc21bed77ceddecf0ec0781fb57abeaa1", 65536) = 119
[pid 687328] getuid()                   = 0
[pid 687328] umount2("", 0x80000000 /* MNT_??? */) = -1 EINVAL (Invalid argument)
```

(Even later:)  That 0x80000000 flag is UMOUNT_UNUSED.  I think this is all in the wrong direction. 

Finally grepped for "chdir" in that strace.  Yep, it's returning EINVAL when trying to change to the ./merged directory -- it tries this four times:
```
[pid 816321] chdir("/home/stevegt/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/dcbd0c051fa67abd6c7c3007e7bffbcbc21bed77ceddecf0ec0781fb57abeaa1/merged" <unfinished ...>
[pid 816321] <... chdir resumed>)       = -1 EINVAL (Invalid argument)
```

This is looking more like a FUSE thing.  I may come back later and add more if I can figure this out; for now, just attaching the entire strace output here:  [chdir-errs.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/6642561/chdir-errs.txt)

The OP's kernel:  ` kernel: 3.10.0-1160.15.2.el7.x86_64`
My kernel was: `4.8.0-58-generic`

Problem went away for me when I upgraded to kernel 5.4.0.  Turns out it's possible, starting with an older image and upgrading packages selectively, to get the host image into a state where the buildah package installation will succeed, but the system does not have all the prerequisites.   (In my case I was using kubic.)

I wasn't able to prove that the 4.8.0 kernel itself was the issue; it could be that, or it could be related to one of the other packages which was replaced by the 5.4.0 upgrade.

**tl;dr**  You need a recent kernel along with its prereqs.  The fact that I was having an issue with 4.8.0 may also be a concern given the 4.0+ advice in buildah installation docs, but we don't know if the 4.8.0 kernel itself was the issue.

Just now found this in https://github.com/containers/fuse-overlayfs/blob/master/README.md:

> Also, please note that, when using fuse-overlayfs from a user namespace (for example, when using rootless podman) a Linux Kernel > v4.18.0 is required.

So yeah, the 4.8.0 kernel I was running isn't supposed to work, let alone the 3.10.0 kernel in OP's case.  

I've submitted PR containers/buildah#3295 to update the buildah docs.

The podman installation instructions don't mention a minimum kernel version at all -- that should probably be fixed too, but it's going to take a little more rehacking, I think.  The Kubic packages apparently don't check kernel version, so a note about kernel version either needs to go both near the Kubic mention and in the source installation section, or somewhere up near the top of  https://podman.io/getting-started/installation
This issue with kernel versions is it ignores kernels like RHEL/CentOS where fixes are back ported to certain kernels.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I believe the docs are updated, reopen if I am mistaken.
